summary: EXP-254 delivers new section 4.2.7 (id=p4-427) — "What else would have to be true for this eclipse to work on the dome model." Geometric trilemma: dome parameters force a ~1.3 km totality circle lasting 0.9 sec, vs the observed 294 km path with 2m18s max totality (NASA Aug 12 2026 Besselian elements). Forces a choice between (1) wrong parameters, (2) parasitic borrowing of globe geometry for station selection, or (3) non-occultation eclipse mechanism that must then explain station-specific localization without the moon's shadow path. Two new SVG illustrations: shadow-cone comparison (Figure 1) and coverage-vs-time at Hartland (Figure 2). Misstatement framing kept in 4.2.2 only per HNOTE-AMENDMENT-001; 4.2.7 forward-references it.

framing_summary.section_position: 4.2.7, inserted between p4-426 and p4-43-2 inside the §4.2 outer wrapper
framing_summary.argument_structure: Parallel to 4.2.6 magnetic-window commitment. 4.2.6 commits us if the magnetic data lands outside Chapman; 4.2.7 commits us if the dome publishes a coherent V51-internal mechanism that produces both observed path width AND station-specific localization without globe geometry.
framing_summary.trilemma: Three options: (1) Geometric — parameters wrong; (2) Borrowed — station selection parasitic on globe geometry; (3) Aetheric — no occultation, then explain localization. Section names option 2 + option 1 parameters as the dome's actual current implicit position and shows it is internally inconsistent.
framing_summary.eclipse_type_correction: Operator draft used "annular"; NASA confirms total (mag 1.0386). Section uses total throughout. Geometric argument structurally identical.
math_corrections_from_operator_draft: R_sun: 25 → 26.3 km (using NASA-precise sun semi-diameter 0.2631° rather than round 0.25°)
Umbra/totality radius formula: operator used (R_S-R_M)*h_moon/D which is the DELTA, not the radius. Corrected to R_M - (R_S-R_M)*h_moon/D, yielding 0.66 km radius (1.3 km diameter), not 22 km.
Annularity duration: operator estimated 15 sec; corrected to 0.9 sec.
Path width: operator estimated 250-300 km; pinned to 294 km from NASA.
Totality duration: operator estimated 2-5 min; pinned to 2m 18.2s max (138.2 sec) from NASA.
framing_summary.structural_conclusion_unchanged: Even with all numerical corrections, the dome shadow is ~1/220 the observed path width and the dome single-observer totality is ~1/150 the observed maximum duration. Operator draft's structural argument is preserved; the order-of-magnitude mismatch is robust.

hero_findings.path_width_ratio: 294 km observed / 1.3 km dome ≈ 226× shorter on dome
hero_findings.duration_ratio: 138.2 sec observed / 0.9 sec dome ≈ 153× shorter on dome
hero_findings.no_path_at_all: Dome model produces a stationary spot (1.3 km totality circle directly under moon) — not a 3,000+ km path tracing across Greenland → Iceland → Spain

curmudgeon_focus: Verify the umbra formula derivation. Check that R_u = R_M - (R_S-R_M)*h_moon/D yields 0.66 km for the cited inputs.
Stress-test the trilemma. Look for a fourth option in the dome's published material — non-Euclidean geometry, time-varying parameters, retro-causal, etc.
Cross-check Hartland 88% peak coverage on Aug 12 2026 against eclipsewise.com or timeanddate.com station-specific data; the SVG figure depends on it.
Re-verify no aetheric eclipse mechanism is published. Re-search formula_runtime.json, model.html, live.html for any non-occultation framework. If we missed one, option 3 of the trilemma needs rework.
Apply DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 design rubric to both SVGs and the section's typography.
Confirm section nesting: p4-427 must be INSIDE the §4.2 outer wrapper (id=p4-42-1), at the same nesting level as p4-426. Verify the decider_instructions string targets the correct insertion point.
Test the kernel of truth. Pre-registration practice DOES deserve credit; verify we have credited the methodology, not the prediction.

fact_verification_completed.nasa_path_width_294_km: Confirmed — Greatest eclipse path width 294.0 km
fact_verification_completed.nasa_totality_max_138_sec: Confirmed — 2m 18.2s = 138.2 sec at point of greatest duration
fact_verification_completed.no_aetheric_eclipse_mechanism_in_v51: Confirmed absent in formula_runtime.json (six formulas; none for eclipse), model.html, live.html as of 2026-04-25 dome content

amendment_compliance.misstatement_framing_in_4_2_7: NOT included in 4.2.7 prose. Section forward-references 4.2.2 in its second paragraph ("This argument is independent of, and complementary to, the misstatement-of-globe-prediction problem we establish at the top of 4.2.2")

notes_for_decider: Three new files to handle: data/sections.json (modify), docs/assets/eclipse-geometry-comparison.svg (create from new_files[0].content), docs/assets/eclipse-coverage-vs-time-hartland.svg (create from new_files[1].content).
